TF-E1.Summary: MVP 1: Foundation — Auth, RBAC, CRUD, Docs Scaffold
TF-E1.Description:
Establish TaskFlow AI greenfield monorepo with Clean Architecture backend, Next.js frontend (Vercel-ready), Supabase PostgreSQL with RLS, JWT authentication, RBAC/ABAC, projects/tasks/comments CRUD, audit logs, OpenTelemetry baseline, and co-located AGENT.md governance.

SCOPE:
- Monorepo scaffold with uv-managed Python backend and Next.js 16 frontend
- Supabase + Alembic migrations with Row-Level Security on all tenant tables
- Authentication: JWT + refresh tokens via Supabase Auth
- RBAC: Admin, Manager, Member roles with ABAC user_id/workspace_id propagation
- API v1: projects, tasks, comments with repository pattern
- Frontend: dashboard, auth flows, task/project management UI
- Security baseline: nh3 (BE), DOMPurify prep, spotlighting utility scaffold
- Observability: structlog + trace_id, OpenTelemetry baseline, /metrics endpoint
- Docs: EXECUTION-RULES.md, PLAN.md, tasks/todo.md, tasks/lessons.md
- CI: GitHub Actions (ruff, mypy, pytest, eslint, docker build)

EFFORT: XL (80 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Full Stack
DEPENDENCIES: None

EPIC LINK: epic/taskflow-implementation
FEATURE BRANCH: epic/TF-E1-foundation

REFERENCE DOCS:
- docs/TaskFlowAI_Project_Proposal.md (§ MVP 1, § Project Structure)
- docs/ENGINEERING-STANDARDS.md (when created)
- docs/SECURITY.md (when created)

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- User can register, login, create project, add tasks, comment on tasks
- RLS prevents cross-tenant data access in integration tests
- Backend verification gate passes: ruff, mypy, pytest >80% coverage
- Root AGENT.md, backend/AGENT.md, frontend/AGENT.md, infrastructure/AGENT.md exist
- docs/EXECUTION-RULES.md and docs/tasks/todo.md populated
- GET /health and GET /metrics return 200

Create the taskflow-ai/ root directory with required base files and AGENT.md index.

USE CASES:
- Developer clones repo and runs uv sync + npm install without errors
- Cursor agent reads AGENT.md at session start for workflow rules
- New contributor finds .env.example with all required variables documented

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create pyproject.toml with uv-managed dependencies (fastapi>=0.115.0, pydantic>=2.8.0, sqlalchemy[asyncio]>=2.0, alembic, uvicorn, structlog, httpx, python-jose, passlib, redis)
- Create .env.example: DATABASE_URL, SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, JWT_SECRET_KEY, REDIS_URL, APP_ENV, APP_DEBUG, OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT, NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL
- Create .gitignore: node_modules, .env, __pycache__, .next, .venv, .uv
- Create root README.md with project overview, hybrid deploy note (Vercel FE + Docker BE)
- Create AGENT.md: workflow rules table, MVP status, doc index per proposal v2.0.0
- Create .cursor/rules/: coding.mdc, testing.mdc, refactor.mdc, docs.mdc (stubs)

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Infrastructure
DEPENDENCIES: None

TESTING CRITERIA:
- All files present at repo root
- uv sync succeeds without errors
- .env.example lists every variable used across full stack
- AGENT.md under 200 lines — index only

EDGE CASES:
- No secrets committed even accidentally
- pyproject.toml uses python>=3.12 constraint
- README documents Vercel cannot host LangGraph/MCP backend

Create docs/ tree with workflow source of truth and task tracking files.

USE CASES:
- Agent writes plan to docs/tasks/todo.md before coding any epic task
- Agent reads docs/tasks/lessons.md at session start to avoid repeated mistakes
- docs/EXECUTION-RULES.md governs all implementation discipline

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create docs/EXECUTION-RULES.md: §1 Production code, §2 ReAct loop, §3 Agent rules (stub), §4 YOLO exceptions, §5 Tech stack, §6 Testing, §7 Doc sync, §8 Context mgmt, §9 Git branch policy
- Create docs/PLAN.md: epic checklist from jira-tickets-json
- Create docs/TOKEN-EFFICIENCY.md: context window discipline
- Create docs/tasks/todo.md, docs/tasks/lessons.md, docs/tasks/checkpoint.md (templates)
- Create docs/tasks/checkpoints/.gitkeep
- Create docs/adr/.gitkeep, docs/learning/.gitkeep, docs/example-code/.gitkeep
- Create docs/guidance/: docker-setup.md, supabase-setup.md, try-it-locally.md (stubs)

EFFORT: M (3 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Documentation
DEPENDENCIES: TF-001

TESTING CRITERIA:
- All docs paths exist and are non-empty where required
- EXECUTION-RULES.md references backend verification gate command
- PLAN.md lists TF-E1 through TF-E7 epics

EDGE CASES:
- checkpoint.md template includes Resume Command section
- lessons.md has Security, Architecture, Testing, Workflow sections

Create backend/main.py with FastAPI application, health endpoint, API v1 router mount, and structured logging.

USE CASES:
- Dev runs uvicorn and hits GET /health for container orchestration probes
- All API routes mounted under /api/v1/ with consistent error responses
- Every log line includes trace_id for request correlation

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/__init__.py, backend/main.py with lifespan context manager
- Create backend/settings.py: pydantic-settings BaseSettings (all env vars from .env.example)
- Create backend/logging_config.py: structlog JSON renderer with trace_id processor
- Create backend/api/v1/router.py: aggregate v1 routers
- Implement GET /health: {status: healthy, version: APP_VERSION}
- Implement GET /metrics: Prometheus stub (expanded in TF-011)
- Add CORS middleware for NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL origin
- Create backend/AGENT.md: envelope schema ref, verification gate, error handling patterns
- Create backend/exceptions.py: AppException hierarchy with structured error responses

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-001

TESTING CRITERIA:
- uvicorn starts without errors
- /health returns 200 JSON
- Structured logs output as JSON with trace_id
- CORS headers present on API responses
- mypy passes on backend/main.py and settings.py

EDGE CASES:
- Settings validation fails fast on missing DATABASE_URL in production mode
- Health endpoint requires no authentication
- APP_DEBUG=false sets log level INFO

Create frontend/ with Next.js 16 App Router, Tailwind, shadcn/ui base, and Vercel deployment config.

USE CASES:
- User visits app and sees TaskFlow AI landing/dashboard shell
- Frontend deploys to Vercel with NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL pointing to Docker backend
- npm run build succeeds for production deployment

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- npx create-next-app@latest frontend --typescript --tailwind --app
- Configure next.config.ts: reactStrictMode true (no standalone — Vercel handles deploy)
- Install shadcn/ui, TanStack Query, React Hook Form, Zod, DOMPurify
- Create app/layout.tsx, app/page.tsx: dashboard shell with TaskFlow branding
- Create frontend/lib/api.ts: typed fetch wrapper with auth header injection
- Create frontend/AGENT.md: component standards, sanitization, WCAG 2.2 AA, Vitest 75% gate
- Create vercel.json if needed for API proxy headers
- Configure Tailwind CSS 4 via @theme in globals.css

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Frontend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-001

TESTING CRITERIA:
- npm run build succeeds
- npm run lint passes
- Dashboard loads at localhost:3000
- api.ts throws typed error on 401

EDGE CASES:
- NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL unset: show config error banner in dev
- Do NOT use output standalone — Vercel native deploy
- DOMPurify imported only in client components

TF-E1.Summary: Supabase Async SQLAlchemy and Alembic Migrations
TF-E1.Description:
Wire Supabase Supavisor (:6543) via async SQLAlchemy with Alembic migrations and RLS-ready schema.

USE CASES:
- Admin runs alembic upgrade head against Supabase project
- Backend persists users, workspaces, projects, tasks, comments via async repository layer
- RLS policies enforce tenant isolation at database level

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Add asyncpg, sqlalchemy[asyncio], alembic to pyproject.toml
- Create backend/db/session.py: prepare_database_url (SSL, statement_cache_size=0 for Supavisor)
- Create backend/db/base.py, backend/db/models.py: User, Workspace, WorkspaceMember, Project, Task, Comment, AuditLog
- Create alembic/ initial migration 001_initial_schema
- Create backend/repositories/base.py: AsyncRepository generic pattern
- Document Supabase RLS policies in docs/guidance/supabase-setup.md
- All tables include workspace_id for tenant scoping

EFFORT: L (6 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Database
DEPENDENCIES: TF-003

TESTING CRITERIA:
- alembic upgrade head succeeds against Supabase
- Models import without circular dependency errors
- Integration test: insert task, query by workspace_id succeeds
- statement_cache_size=0 documented for Supavisor

EDGE CASES:
- Password special characters in DATABASE_URL: quote in .env
- Migration rollback: downgrade head works cleanly
- UUID primary keys on all entities

Implement JWT authentication with refresh token rotation and Supabase Auth integration.

USE CASES:
- User registers with email/password and receives access + refresh tokens
- User logs in and accesses protected /api/v1/projects endpoints
- Expired access token refreshed via POST /api/v1/auth/refresh without re-login
- Invalid token returns 401 with structured error

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/api/v1/auth.py: register, login, refresh, logout, me endpoints
- Create backend/services/auth_service.py: password hashing (passlib bcrypt), JWT create/verify (python-jose)
- Create backend/schemas/auth.py: RegisterRequest, LoginRequest, TokenResponse, UserResponse
- Create backend/dependencies/auth.py: get_current_user dependency extracting user_id from JWT
- Refresh token rotation: invalidate old refresh on use
- Create frontend/app/login/page.tsx, frontend/app/register/page.tsx
- Store tokens in httpOnly cookies or secure localStorage per security review

EFFORT: L (8 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Frontend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-005

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Register → login → me returns user profile
- Expired access token + valid refresh returns new pair
- Invalid JWT returns 401
- Password < 8 chars rejected with 422
- test_auth.py covers happy path and 5 edge cases

EDGE CASES:
- Duplicate email registration: 409 Conflict
- Refresh token reuse after rotation: 401
- Logout invalidates refresh token server-side
- Brute force: rate limit login endpoint (10/min per IP)

Implement role-based access control with attribute-based policies on all data paths.

USE CASES:
- Admin invites users and manages workspace settings
- Manager creates projects and assigns tasks to team members
- Member can only edit own tasks and comment on accessible projects
- User cannot access another workspace's data (IDOR prevention)

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Define Role enum: ADMIN, MANAGER, MEMBER on WorkspaceMember model
- Create backend/security/abac.py: check_permission(user, resource, action) -> bool
- Create backend/dependencies/rbac.py: require_role(*roles) decorator
- Permission matrix per proposal: Admin (full), Manager (project CRUD), Member (own tasks)
- Propagate user_id and workspace_id from JWT through service → repository → query filter
- Add workspace_id to all repository list/get methods as mandatory filter
- Create backend/tests/test_rbac.py and test_idor.py

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Security
DEPENDENCIES: TF-006

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Member cannot delete project (403)
- Manager can assign task to member in same workspace
- Cross-workspace task access returns 404 not 403 (no leak)
- ABAC unit tests cover all role × action combinations

EDGE CASES:
- User removed from workspace mid-session: next request 403
- Admin demoted to member: permissions update immediately
- Missing workspace_id in JWT: 401

Implement Clean Architecture CRUD for projects and tasks with service and repository layers.

USE CASES:
- Manager creates project with name and description
- User creates task with title, description, priority, due_date, assignee
- User lists tasks filtered by project, status, priority
- User updates task status (todo → in_progress → done)
- User soft-deletes task (archived, not hard delete)

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/api/v1/projects.py, backend/api/v1/tasks.py
- Create backend/services/project_service.py, backend/services/task_service.py
- Create backend/repositories/project_repository.py, backend/repositories/task_repository.py
- Create backend/schemas/project.py, backend/schemas/task.py (Pydantic v2 strict)
- Task fields: id, project_id, title, description, status, priority, due_date, assignee_id, created_by, workspace_id
- Priority enum: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, URGENT; Status enum: TODO, IN_PROGRESS, DONE, ARCHIVED
- Pagination: limit/offset on list endpoints
- nh3 sanitization on description fields in service layer

EFFORT: L (8 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-007

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Full CRUD cycle for project and task in integration test
- List tasks by project returns only workspace-scoped tasks
- Invalid priority enum returns 422
- Sanitized description strips script tags
- Repository pattern: no SQL in service layer

EDGE CASES:
- Create task in non-existent project: 404
- Assignee not in workspace: 422
- due_date in past: allowed with warning in response metadata
- Empty title: 422 validation error

Implement task comments CRUD with backend nh3 and frontend DOMPurify sanitization.

USE CASES:
- User adds comment on accessible task
- User edits own comment within 15 minutes of creation
- User views comment thread on task detail page
- Malicious HTML in comment body is stripped, not executed

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/api/v1/comments.py, backend/services/comment_service.py
- Create backend/repositories/comment_repository.py, backend/schemas/comment.py
- Sanitize body with nh3.clean() allowing safe tags only (p, br, strong, em, code)
- Create frontend/components/CommentThread.tsx with DOMPurify.sanitize before render
- Comments linked to task_id with workspace_id denormalized for RLS
- Author can edit/delete own comments; Manager can delete any in workspace

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Frontend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-008

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Comment CRUD integration test passes
- <script>alert(1)</script> stripped in API response
- DOMPurify test: rendered HTML contains no script tags
- Non-author edit attempt returns 403

EDGE CASES:
- Comment on archived task: 409 Conflict
- Empty comment body: 422
- Very long comment (>10000 chars): 422
- Deleted task: comments return 404 on new comment

Build dashboard UI with project overview, task list, task detail, and forms.

USE CASES:
- User sees dashboard with project cards and overdue task count
- User creates/edits task via modal form with Zod validation
- User navigates project → task list → task detail with comments
- Loading and error states shown via TanStack Query

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create frontend/components/Dashboard.tsx, ProjectCard.tsx, TaskList.tsx, TaskForm.tsx
- Create frontend/app/projects/[id]/page.tsx, frontend/app/tasks/[id]/page.tsx
- TanStack Query hooks: useProjects, useTasks, useComments with optimistic updates
- React Hook Form + Zod schemas mirroring backend Pydantic models
- shadcn/ui: Button, Dialog, Input, Select, Badge for priority/status
- WCAG 2.2 AA: keyboard nav, aria labels on forms

EFFORT: L (10 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Frontend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-004, TF-008, TF-009

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Vitest component tests for TaskForm validation
- E2E happy path: login → create project → create task → add comment
- Error boundary catches API failures gracefully
- Mobile responsive layout at 375px width

EDGE CASES:
- Empty project list: show onboarding CTA
- Network error on save: toast with retry
- Concurrent edit conflict: show latest server state
- Session expired mid-form: redirect to login

Implement append-only audit trail and OpenTelemetry trace_id propagation with Prometheus /metrics.

USE CASES:
- Admin views audit log of auth events and task changes
- Every API request has trace_id in logs and response header
- Ops scrapes /metrics for api_request_duration_seconds histogram

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/services/audit_service.py: log_event(actor_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, metadata)
- Audit events: user.login, user.register, task.created, task.updated, task.deleted, comment.created
- Create backend/telemetry.py: configure_opentelemetry(), get_tracer()
- Middleware: bind trace_id to structlog context, set X-Trace-Id response header
- Create backend/metrics.py: api_request_duration_seconds, http_requests_total
- Mount prometheus /metrics on FastAPI (no auth required)
- Create backend/security/spotlighting.py: spotlight_external_content() scaffold for MVP 3

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Observability
DEPENDENCIES: TF-003

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Task create generates audit log entry with correct actor_id
- trace_id consistent in logs for single request
- /metrics returns Prometheus text format
- spotlighting wraps content in <<<EXTERNAL_CONTENT>>> markers

EDGE CASES:
- OTLP endpoint unavailable: log warning, continue without export
- Audit log write failure: log error, do not fail main request
- PII in audit metadata: hash or omit sensitive fields

TF-E1.Summary: GitHub Actions CI and Docker Backend Scaffold
TF-E1.Description:
Create CI pipeline and multi-stage Dockerfile for backend (frontend deploys to Vercel separately).

USE CASES:
- PR to epic/taskflow-implementation triggers lint, typecheck, test jobs
- Docker image builds for backend API deployment to GHCR
- infrastructure/AGENT.md documents CI/CD rules

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create .github/workflows/ci.yml: backend (uv sync, ruff, mypy, pytest), frontend (npm ci, lint, build), docker build
- Create infrastructure/Dockerfile: python:3.12-slim, uv sync, copy backend/, EXPOSE 8010
- Create infrastructure/docker-compose.yml: app + redis services for local dev
- Create infrastructure/nginx.conf: proxy /api to FastAPI (for local hybrid test)
- Create infrastructure/AGENT.md: CI gates, Cosign placeholder for MVP 6
- Cache uv and npm in CI
- workflow-docs job: verify docs/tasks/todo.md and lessons.md exist

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Infrastructure, CI/CD
DEPENDENCIES: TF-003, TF-004

TESTING CRITERIA:
- All CI jobs pass on clean codebase
- docker build succeeds
- docker-compose up: /health returns 200
- Failed ruff check fails PR

EDGE CASES:
- Docker build without frontend artifact: backend-only image
- CI runs on push to epic/taskflow-implementation and PRs
- mypy strict mode: zero errors on backend/
